title: CoSBC: Dependence-Specialized Enriched SBC with Symmetric Pooled Ranking
description: CoSBC is a dependence-specialized instantiation of enriched simulation-based calibration rather than a new calibration framework. It pools the planted draw with posterior draws, computes permutation-equivariant multivariate scores on covariance and tail feature families, and uses Monte Carlo randomization to calibrate a global max-over-families test under a CPU-feasible budget.
motivation: Standard SBC is exact but can miss dependence failures because it is usually applied to coordinates or a few scalar summaries. The real gap is narrower than a new method family: a fair test of whether symmetric pooled ranking adds power beyond feature-matched enriched SBC when both methods use the same dependence summaries and the same aggregation/calibration scheme, plus evidence that the diagnostic matters in at least one realistic approximate posterior setting.
proposed_approach: For each SBC or posterior-SBC replicate, CoSBC pools the planted draw with posterior draws, applies randomized pooled coordinate ranks, maps them to copula or Gaussianized coordinates, and forms two pre-registered feature families: pairwise covariance products and upper/lower tail co-occurrence indicators. It then computes a leave-one-out multivariate energy score for each candidate, ranks the planted candidate symmetrically within each family, and calibrates family and global statistics with plus-one Monte Carlo randomization over within-replicate relabelings, using exhaustive enumeration only in a tiny implementation check.

related_work.title: Validating Bayesian Inference Algorithms with Simulation-Based Calibration
related_work.authors: Sean Talts, Michael Betancourt, Daniel Simpson, Aki Vehtari, Andrew Gelman
related_work.how_it_differs: Foundational SBC paper. CoSBC keeps the same exchangeability logic and changes only the test-quantity construction and matched global calibration.

related_work.title: Simulation-Based Calibration Checking for Bayesian Computation: The Choice of Test Quantities Shapes Sensitivity
related_work.authors: Martin Modrak, Angie H. Moon, Shinyoung Kim, Paul-Christian Burkner, Niko Huurre, Katerina Faltejskova, Andrew Gelman, Aki Vehtari
related_work.how_it_differs: Closest precursor. CoSBC is explicitly framed as a dependence-specialized enriched-SBC instantiation and must beat a feature-matched enriched-SBC baseline to justify a stronger novelty claim.

related_work.title: Discriminative Calibration: Check Bayesian Computation from Simulations and Flexible Classifier
related_work.how_it_differs: Provides a broader multivariate diagnostic through classification. CoSBC is narrower, rank-based, and designed to preserve SBC-style finite-sample validity for each feature family.

related_work.title: Posterior SBC: Simulation-Based Calibration Checking Conditional on Data
related_work.authors: Teemu Sailynoja, Marvin Schmitt, Paul-Christian Burkner, Aki Vehtari
related_work.how_it_differs: Supplies the conditional-on-data framework that CoSBC reuses for a realistic fixed-data study rather than replacing.

related_work.title: A Targeted Accuracy Diagnostic for Variational Approximations
related_work.authors: Yu Wang, Mikolaj Kasprzak, Jonathan H. Huggins
related_work.how_it_differs: Motivates targeted diagnostics for approximate posteriors and helps position CoSBC as a dependence-focused check rather than an omnibus accuracy metric.

related_work.title: Diagnostics for Variational Bayes Approximations
related_work.how_it_differs: Earlier covariance-oriented diagnostic work for approximate posteriors. CoSBC differs by using SBC exchangeability and randomization-calibrated ranks instead of VB-specific correction heuristics.

related_work.title: A Kernel Two-Sample Test
related_work.authors: Arthur Gretton, Karsten M. Borgwardt, Malte J. Rasch, Bernhard Scholkopf, Alex J. Smola
related_work.how_it_differs: Contributes discrepancy ideas used only as optional score ingredients inside the SBC ranking construction.

related_work.title: Energy Statistics: A Class of Statistics Based on Distances
related_work.authors: Gabor J. Szekely, Maria L. Rizzo
related_work.how_it_differs: Provides the distance-based score family used in the main pooled ranking construction.

related_work.title: Copula Discrepancy: Benchmarking Dependence Structure
related_work.how_it_differs: Motivates dependence-specific diagnostics from a copula perspective but does not study SBC exchangeability or matched enriched-SBC comparisons.

hypothesis: When covariance and tail summaries, family aggregation, and global calibration are held fixed, symmetric pooled multivariate ranking improves detection or localization of dependence failures over feature-matched enriched SBC, while remaining calibrated under exact inference and useful in at least one realistic posterior approximation study.
success_criteria: Under exact inference, per-family CoSBC ranks remain uniform, and a tiny exhaustive check with R=4 and M=7 matches the Monte Carlo randomization implementation.
The feature-matched enriched-SBC baseline uses the same pooled transforms, the same covariance and tail feature bank, and the same randomization-calibrated family/global aggregation as CoSBC.

CoSBC exceeds the feature-matched enriched-SBC baseline by at least 0.08 absolute power at alpha=0.05 on at least two synthetic dependence-only alternatives and by at least 0.05 on at least one realistic posterior-SBC alternative; otherwise the stronger novelty claim is dropped.

The realistic fixed-data study uses posterior SBC on a small hierarchical real-data model, comparing NUTS against practical approximations such as mean-field ADVI and Laplace, to show relevance beyond conjugate Gaussian benchmarks.

The complete study fits within 3 seeds, 100 replicates per main setting, 24 posterior draws per replicate, 199 Monte Carlo relabelings for main tests, and an overall budget of about 8 CPU-hours on 2 cores.
